verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation, with SH2/SH3/kinase domains facing the cytosol. Its protein body is not exposed extracellularly. Checking contextual buckets: no documented cancer- or cell-death-induced outer-leaflet exposure of the SRC protein body; no narrow germline/developmental ecto-display; not a lysosomal TM protein, so no lysosomal exocytosis route; no documented PM minority pool on the outer face or cycling to extracellular surface; no wash-resistant covalent tethering to a TM partner that would expose it extracellularly. Clinical SRC inhibitors (dasatinib, bosutinib) are intracellular ATP-pocket binders, consistent with cytoplasmic-face localization.
